subject: Chemistry
title: Why Deodorant Stops Smell
description: A fast breakdown of Why Deodorant Stops Smell for builders.

hook.text: Sweat is odorless. The smell comes from bacteria on your skin breaking down sweat into stinky acids.

buildup.text: Apocrine glands in armpits produce protein-rich sweat. Bacteria feast on it and release thioalcohols.

discovery.text: Deodorants use antimicrobials to kill bacteria. Antiperspirants use aluminum salts to block sweat glands.

twist.text: Aluminum chloride forms a gel plug inside sweat ducts, physically preventing sweat from reaching the surface.

climax.text: Fragrance masks remaining odor. It's a three-layer chemical defense: kill, block, and cover.

punchline.text: Kill the bacteria, block the sweat, mask the rest. Chemistry vs. body odor.

quiz.question: What actually causes body odor?
options: Bacteria on skin breaking down sweat into acids
Sweat itself has a strong natural smell
Dead skin cells decomposing on the surface
